rationale: Ground truth centers on two threads: (1) the seed-reproducibility regression for talker_mtp under full cudagraphs (gcanlin's 'mtp seed failed when decode batch > 1'), and (2) the NPU deploy config needing cudagraph_mode: PIECEWISE to avoid a crash (Wallbreazzz). Y explicitly surfaces both — flagging the missing accuracy A/B given the seeding change and questioning whether the NPU PIECEWISE addition was applied correctly to both stages — while X never mentions seeding or the NPU config at all. X's major findings (OOM risk from max_num_seqs 64, missing tests for graph-wrapped/async paths) are grounded in the diff but are speculative and partly contradicted by the PR's own benchmark evidence (256/256 requests completed with no OOM). Both candidates independently catch the two stale-comment issues (prefix caching, batch-token header) with good file/line specificity, and both are reasonably actionable, but Y's findings track the actual human review concerns far more closely.
